Cytoplasmic Inheritance: Viruses
More modern applications of cytoplasmic inheritance…..
Studies of mtDNA have led to the concepts of  the Molecular Clock, and Most Recent Common Ancestor for studying human evolution

Cytoplasmic Genes
Code for respiratory genes (mitochondria) and photosynthetic genes (chloroplast), as well as genes involved in protein synthesis, (rRNA and tRNA)
Are generally present in high copy number
(useful for studying ancient samples/Evolution)
Are generally on a circular genome
Know comparison of yeast and human
Genetic analysis possible
in yeast, a facultative aerobe, for mito
chlamydomonas for chloroplast
Weird
Most exhibit uniparental inheritance
May have their own unique genetic code
May exhibit RNA editing
May be intracellular bacteria, or viruses
Mitochondrial Eve, molecular clock

Yeast mitochondrial mutants
Mitochondrial mutants die on a 3 carbon source such as glycerol
Mutations that effect yeast respiration can survive on Glucose plates
Facultative aerobe
